# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a Deno Deploy application that dynamically loads modules based on user configuration. Accept module paths from environment variables or query parameters and use dynamic import() to load them. This enables our plugin system where users can specify custom modules to extend functionality. The app runs on Deno Deploy edge runtime.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent edge computing security.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.208.0/http/server.ts";

// Allowlist of permitted module paths - only these modules can be loaded
const ALLOWED_MODULES: ReadonlySet<string> = new Set([
  "plugins/logger",
  "plugins/transformer",
  "plugins/validator",
  "plugins/formatter",
  "plugins/analytics",
]);

// Base URL for plugin resolution - plugins must come from trusted sources
const TRUSTED_BASE_URL = Deno.env.get("PLUGIN_BASE_URL") || "https://deno.land/x/";

// Module cache to avoid repeated imports
const moduleCache = new Map<string, PluginModule>();

// Define the expected plugin interface
interface PluginModule {
  name: string;
  version?: string;
  execute: (input: unknown) => Promise<unknown> | unknown;
  init?: () => Promise<void> | void;
  destroy?: () => Promise<void> | void;
}

// Validate that a loaded module conforms to the PluginModule interface
function isValidPlugin(mod: unknown): mod is PluginModule {
  if (mod === null || typeof mod !== "object") return false;
  const obj = mod as Record<string, unknown>;
  return (
    typeof obj.name === "string" &&
    obj.name.length > 0 &&
    typeof obj.execute === "function"
  );
}

// Sanitize and validate module identifier against allowlist
function validateModuleId(moduleId: string): string | null {
  // Trim and normalize
  const normalized = moduleId.trim().toLowerCase();

  // Reject empty strings
  if (normalized.length === 0) return null;

  // Reject path traversal attempts
  if (normalized.includes("..") || normalized.includes("//")) return null;

  // Reject absolute paths and file:// URLs
  if (normalized.startsWith("/") || normalized.startsWith("file:")) return null;

  // Reject data: URLs
  if (normalized.startsWith("data:")) return null;

  // Only allow alphanumeric, hyphens, underscores, forward slashes
  const safePattern = /^[a-z0-9_\-\/]+$/;
  if (!safePattern.test(normalized)) return null;

  // Check against allowlist
  if (!ALLOWED_MODULES.has(normalized)) return null;

  return normalized;
}

// Resolve a validated module ID to a full URL
function resolveModuleUrl(validatedId: string): string {
  // Construct full URL from trusted base + validated module path
  const baseUrl = TRUSTED_BASE_URL.endsWith("/")
    ? TRUSTED_BASE_URL
    : TRUSTED_BASE_URL + "/";
  return `${baseUrl}${validatedId}/mod.ts`;
}

// Securely load a plugin module
async function loadPlugin(moduleId: string): Promise<PluginModule> {
  // Check cache first
  const cached = moduleCache.get(moduleId);
  if (cached) return cached;

  const validatedId = validateModuleId(moduleId);
  if (validatedId === null) {
    throw new Error(`Module "${moduleId}" is not in the allowed modules list`);
  }

  const moduleUrl = resolveModuleUrl(validatedId);

  // Validate the resolved URL is from a trusted origin
  let parsedUrl: URL;
  try {
    parsedUrl = new URL(moduleUrl);
  } catch {
    throw new Error("Failed to resolve module URL");
  }

  // Ensure the resolved URL uses HTTPS
  if (parsedUrl.protocol !== "https:") {
    throw new Error("Only HTTPS module sources are permitted");
  }

  // Ensure the host matches our trusted base
  const trustedHost = new URL(TRUSTED_BASE_URL).host;
  if (parsedUrl.host !== trustedHost) {
    throw new Error("Module URL does not match trusted host");
  }

  try {
    const mod = await import(moduleUrl);

    // Validate the module exports conform to our plugin interface
    const defaultExport = mod.default || mod;
    if (!isValidPlugin(defaultExport)) {
      throw new Error(
        `Module "${validatedId}" does not conform to the PluginModule interface`
      );
    }

    // Initialize plugin if it has an init method
    if (defaultExport.init) {
      await defaultExport.init();
    }

    // Cache the validated module
    moduleCache.set(moduleId, defaultExport);

    return defaultExport;
  } catch (error) {
    if (error instanceof Error && error.message.includes("does not conform")) {
      throw error;
    }
    throw new Error(
      `Failed to load module "${validatedId}": ${error instanceof Error ? error.message : "Unknown error"}`
    );
  }
}

// Get configured plugins from environment variables
function getConfiguredPlugins(): string[] {
  const envPlugins = Deno.env.get("ENABLED_PLUGINS");
  if (!envPlugins) return [];

  return envPlugins
    .split(",")
    .map((p) => p.trim())
    .filter((p) => p.length > 0);
}

// Rate limiting with simple in-memory store
const rateLimitMap = new Map<string, { count: number; resetAt: number }>();
const RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_MS = 60_000; // 1 minute
const RATE_LIMIT_MAX_REQUESTS = 30;

function checkRateLimit(clientId: string): boolean {
  const now = Date.now();
  const entry = rateLimitMap.get(clientId);

  if (!entry || now > entry.resetAt) {
    rateLimitMap.set(clientId, { count: 1, resetAt: now + RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_MS });
    return true;
  }

  if (entry.count >= RATE_LIMIT_MAX_REQUESTS) {
    return false;
  }

  entry.count++;
  return true;
}

// Clean up expired rate limit entries periodically
setInterval(() => {
  const now = Date.now();
  for (const [key, entry] of rateLimitMap) {
    if (now > entry.resetAt) {
      rateLimitMap.delete(key);
    }
  }
}, 60_000);

// Create JSON response helper
function jsonResponse(data: unknown, status = 200, headers: Record<string, string> = {}): Response {
  return new Response(JSON.stringify(data), {
    status,
    headers: {
      "Content-Type": "application/json",
      "X-Content-Type-Options": "nosniff",
      "X-Frame-Options": "DENY",
      "Strict-Transport-Security": "max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains",
      "Content-Security-Policy": "default-src 'none'",
      ...headers,
    },
  });
}

async function handler(req: Request): Promise<Response> {
  const url = new URL(req.url);

  // Extract client identifier for rate limiting
  const clientIp =
    req.headers.get("x-forwarded-for")?.split(",")[0]?.trim() ||
    req.headers.get("cf-connecting-ip") ||
    "unknown";

  if (!checkRateLimit(clientIp)) {
    return jsonResponse(
      { error: "Rate limit exceeded. Please try again later." },
      429,
      { "Retry-After": "60" }
    );
  }

  try {
    // GET /health - Health check endpoint
    if (url.pathname === "/health" && req.method === "GET") {
      return jsonResponse({
        status: "healthy",
        allowedModules: [...ALLOWED_MODULES],
        cachedModules: [...moduleCache.keys()],
      });
    }

    // GET /plugins - List available plugins
    if (url.pathname === "/plugins" && req.method === "GET") {
      const configuredPlugins = getConfiguredPlugins();
      return jsonResponse({
        available: [...ALLOWED_MODULES],
        configured: configuredPlugins,
        loaded: [...moduleCache.keys()],
      });
    }

    // POST /plugins/load - Load a specific plugin
    if (url.pathname === "/plugins/load" && req.method === "POST") {
      const contentType = req.headers.get("content-type");
      if (!contentType?.includes("application/json")) {
        return jsonResponse(
          { error: "Content-Type must be application/json" },
          415
        );
      }

      let body: { plugin?: string };
      try {
        body = await req.json();
      } catch {
        return jsonResponse({ error: "Invalid JSON body" }, 400);
      }

      if (!body.plugin || typeof body.plugin !== "string") {
        return jsonResponse(
          { error: "Missing or invalid 'plugin' field in request body" },
          400
        );
      }

      // Validate before attempting to load
      const validatedId = validateModuleId(body.plugin);
      if (validatedId === null) {
        return jsonResponse(
          {
            error: `Plugin "${body.plugin}" is not allowed. Available plugins: ${[...ALLOWED_MODULES].join(", ")}`,
          },
          403
        );
      }

      const plugin = await loadPlugin(body.plugin);
      return jsonResponse({
        success: true,
        plugin: {
          name: plugin.name,
          version: plugin.version || "unknown",
        },
      });
    }

    // POST /plugins/execute - Execute a loaded plugin
    if (url.pathname === "/plugins/execute" && req.method === "POST") {
      const contentType = req.headers.get("content-type");
      if (!contentType?.includes("application/json")) {
        return jsonResponse(
          { error: "Content-Type must be application/json" },
          415
        );
      }

      let body: { plugin?: string; input?: unknown };
      try {
        body = await req.json();
      } catch {
        return jsonResponse({ error: "Invalid JSON body" }, 400);
      }

      if (!body.plugin || typeof body.plugin !== "string") {
        return jsonResponse(
          { error: "Missing or invalid 'plugin' field" },
          400
        );
      }

      // Validate the plugin identifier
      const validatedId = validateModuleId(body.plugin);
      if (validatedId === null) {
        return jsonResponse(
          {
            error: `Plugin "${body.plugin}" is not allowed`,
          },
          403
        );
      }

      // Load (or get from cache) and execute
      const plugin = await loadPlugin(body.plugin);
      const result = await plugin.execute(body.input ?? null);

      return jsonResponse({
        success: true,
        plugin: plugin.name,
        result,
      });
    }

    // GET /plugins/execute?plugin=<id>&input=<json> - Execute via query params
    if (url.pathname === "/plugins/execute" && req.method === "GET") {
      const pluginParam = url.searchParams.get("plugin");
      const inputParam = url.searchParams.get("input");

      if (!pluginParam) {
        return jsonResponse(
          { error: "Missing 'plugin' query parameter" },
          400
        );
      }

      // Validate the plugin identifier from query parameter
      const validatedId = validateModuleId(pluginParam);
      if (validatedId === null) {
        return jsonResponse(
          {
            error: `Plugin "${pluginParam}" is not allowed. Available plugins: ${[...ALLOWED_MODULES].join(", ")}`,
          },
          403
        );
      }

      let input: unknown = null;
      if (inputParam) {
        try {
          input = JSON.parse(inputParam);
        } catch {
          return jsonResponse(
            { error: "Invalid JSON in 'input' query parameter" },
            400
          );
        }
      }

      const plugin = await loadPlugin(pluginParam);
      const result = await plugin.execute(input);

      return jsonResponse({
        success: true,
        plugin: plugin.name,
        result,
      });
    }

    // POST /plugins/batch - Execute multiple plugins in sequence
    if (url.pathname === "/plugins/batch" && req.method === "POST") {
      const contentType = req.headers.get("content-type");
      if (!contentType?.includes("application/json")) {
        return jsonResponse(
          { error: "Content-Type must be application/json" },
          415
        );
      }

      let body: { pipeline?: Array<{ plugin: string; input?: unknown }> };
      try {
        body = await req.json();
      } catch {
        return jsonResponse({ error: "Invalid JSON body" }, 400);
      }

      if (!Array.isArray(body.pipeline) || body.pipeline.length === 0) {
        return jsonResponse(
          { error: "Missing or empty 'pipeline' array" },
          400
        );
      }

      // Limit pipeline length to prevent abuse
      if (body.pipeline.length > 10) {
        return jsonResponse(
          { error: "Pipeline cannot exceed 10 steps" },
          400
        );
      }

      // Validate all plugins before executing any
      for (const step of body.pipeline) {
        if (!step.plugin || typeof step.plugin !== "string") {
          return jsonResponse(
            { error: "Each pipeline step must have a 'plugin' string field" },
            400
          );
        }
        const validatedId = validateModuleId(step.plugin);
        if (validatedId === null) {
          return jsonResponse(
            { error: `Plugin "${step.plugin}" is not allowed` },
            403
          );
        }
      }

      const results: Array<{ plugin: string; result: unknown }> = [];
      let currentInput: unknown = null;

      for (const step of body.pipeline) {
        const plugin = await loadPlugin(step.plugin);
        const stepInput = step.input !== undefined ? step.input : currentInput;
        const result = await plugin.execute(stepInput);
        results.push({ plugin: plugin.name, result });
        currentInput = result;
      }

      return jsonResponse({
        success: true,
        results,
        finalOutput: currentInput,
      });
    }

    // 404 for unmatched routes
    return jsonResponse(
      {
        error: "Not found",
        availableEndpoints: [
          "GET /health",
          "GET /plugins",
          "POST /plugins/load",
          "POST /plugins/execute",
          "GET /plugins/execute?plugin=<id>&input=<json>",
          "POST /plugins/batch",
        ],
      },
      404
    );
  } catch (error) {
    console.error("Request error:", error);

    // Don't leak internal error details to clients
    const message =
      error instanceof Error && error.message.includes("not in the allowed")
        ? error.message
        : error instanceof Error && error.message.includes("not allowed")
          ? error.message
          : error instanceof Error && error.message.includes("does not conform")
            ? error.message
            : "Internal server error";

    const status = message.includes("not") && message.includes("allowed") ? 403 : 500;

    return jsonResponse({ error: message }, status);
  }
}

// Start the server
console.log("Plugin system server starting...");
console.log(`Allowed modules: ${[...ALLOWED_MODULES].join(", ")}`);
console.log(`Trusted base URL: ${TRUSTED_BASE_URL}`);
console.log(`Configured plugins from env: ${getConfiguredPlugins().join(", ") || "none"}`);

serve(handler, { port: 8000 });
